rulings.Case Title: KIM MCINTYRE VS. SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 26, 2008 LINE 7. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THE DESIGN CAUSED THE ACCIDENT, THE EXISTENCE OF A DANGEROUS CONDITION AND ACTUAL OR CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE GREENE DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED, EXCEPT FOR OBJECTION EIGHT, WHICH IS SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO THE PFEIFFER DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DICKSON ADETUYI VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 26, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND ALEXANDER HINES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/summary adjudication is denied. There are triable issues of fact as to each of plaintiff's claims: sexual harassment, retaliation and failure to prevent retaliation. The facts include: issue 2, facts 2, 3 and 5; issue 4, fact 16; dress code, facts 1, 3, 4, 7 and 10; writeup, facts 2 and 3; 2006 confrontation, facts 1 and 3; additional allegations, fact 1; III, fact 2; IV, facts 1 and 2 and V, facts 1 and 2. Plaintiff also sets forth numerous disputed facts in Additional Material Facts. As to the statute of limitations, the Court finds that plaintiff's claims are not barred because sufficient facts have been presented to apply the continuing violations doctrine. As contrasted with the decisions in Morgan v. Regents (2000) 88 Cal.App.4th 52 and Cucuzza v. City of Santa Clara (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 1031, the facts at issue do not establish the requisite level of permance to invoke the statute. The acts of harassment and retaliation were arguably on and off events and plaintiff's status on the job was not materially changed. The recent Supreme Court ruling in Yanowitz v. L'Oreal. Inc. (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1028 applies the doctrine to both harassment and retaliation claims and there is sufficient evidence to do so in this case. The GSA settlement is limited to the subject of the grievance and is not a bar to plaintiff's claim. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: YOSHABEL CLEMENTS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Approval Of Filing Ntc Of Pendency Of Action And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 26, 2008 LINE 18. PLAINTIFF YOSHABEL CLEMENTS' MOTION For Approval Of Filing NOTICE Of Pendency Of Action (REQUEST For Judicial NOTICE) IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT APPEAR TO HAVE AN ENFORCEABLE INTEREST IN THE SUBJECT PROPERTY. MOREOVER, THE ORDER REQUESTED WOULD BE CONTRARY TO TH COURT'S ORDER OF AUGUST 31, 2007 ISSUED BY JUDGE DONDERO AND THE RECEIVERSHIP. ANY RELIEF FROM THOSE ORDERS MUST BE BROUGHT BEFORE JUDGE DONDERO. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: EXIGEN, LTD. VS. DIMITRI VAYNBLAT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Arbitration & Mtn To Stay Litigation On Xcomplaint; Appendix Of Ono-California Authorities
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 26, 2008 LINE 11. PLAINTIFFS EXIGEN, LTD. AND GREGORY SHENKMAN'S MOTION To Compel Arbitration & MOTION To Stay Litigation On CROSS-complainT IS DENIED. THE UNSECURED NOTE EXPRESSLY REFERENCES CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S BONUSES. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THAT WILL BE AN ISSUE IN THE NOTE CASE AND DEPENDING ON THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED AND THE DECISION RENDERED, THERE MAY BE NO NEED FOR THE ARBITRATION. THERE ALSO IS THE POSSIBILITY OF CONFLICTING RULINGS IF BOTH THE LITIGATION AND THE ARBITRATION SIMULTANEOUSLY PROCEED. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: MAURIZIO BARONE VS. SONDRA KOHN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 26, 2008 LINE 13. DEFENDANTS SONDRA KOHN, SONDRA KOHN, ANEU PRO SKIN AND LASER CENTER AND SAETA HOLDINGS, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT CONFIRMS PLAINTIFF'S KNOWLEDGE OF THE ADMINISTRATION OF THE ESTATE IN JUNE 2006. UNDER PROBATE CODE SECTION 9100, PLAINTIFF WAS REQUIRED TO FILE A CLAIM WITHIN THE LATER OF FOUR MONTHS AFTER THE APPOINTMENT OF A REPRESENTATIVE OR SIXTY DAYS. BOTH PERIODS HAVE LONG SINCE PASSED. =(302/PJM/AY)
